author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
I love this, I hope people take your opinions, sage advice and humour, on board...

Life is too damned short to be honest, and too many wars have been started over
 "Big end or Little ends"!

(It's Obviously Little end,1st, Lmao!!)
I'm glad, despite occasionally disagreeing with some of the ideas presented here,
    that, safe in the knowledge that I'm Right
, I still enjoy your channel immensely!! 🤔😏😁😅🤣

Seriously, you're an absolute legend, I wish I'd had you as a Teacher, I would've learned how to Think for Myself much earlier than I did!! 🙂
Have a Beautiful Weekend, and I send my
~Kindest Regards,
Andrea, Jasper and George the Pigeon. XxX.

author: @তুহিন_জানা
text:
I'm an IT guy by profession & have been watching the crypto stuff for a while.
The block chain technology that crypto is built upon is excellent & can be very useful for not storing all the data of some app or service in one central data store, but distributing to all users. So one server failing does not shutdown the whole service. But the currencies themselves operate like ponzi schemes. Initial investors buy for cheap, sell to the next idiots for higher price. Then the perceived speculative value collapses, the ones who bought it last take the L.

author: @TerryGilsenan
text: thats excellent. I have tried to say similar things before, but I was loudly shouted down with "Yeah, well thats just your opinion too!" and you know what? they are also correct. So what to do? Well, we should let other people speak, however we have no overriding responsibility to listen. If we want people to listen, then we need to be respectful. and this is a two way street.

author: @czechyorker
text: Reminds me of an argument with people that believe that the earth is a spinning ball. Yet they are not able to provide a real-world example that is it spinning at 1000 mph. Once t was my belief as well and I would defend it. Until one day I decided to go outside and prove it to myself. What I found didn't want to go inside of my brain for a few days. Then it sank in. Just use a high zoom camera and you will see that the math supports it. Yep, it's flat. 8 in per mile square ;-)

author: @richysee
text: An interesting philosophy. I prefer to only offer opinion on what I've proved to be true and correct and I withdraw from discussions involving unproven or irreconcilable fact. But then I'm the product of working for an employer at a level where lives depended on the accuracy of my decision-making. I worked for an international airline for a great many years.

author: @totherarf
text:
Isn't language interesting!
When you say "argue" ..... you really mean discuss!
The point of a discussion is the free and open exchange of ideas. An Argument on the other hand is the imposition of someone's point of view over another!

Personally I think I am right about virtually everything, BUT ... I am willing to listen as to why I am mistaken in my opinion! ;o)

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
"It seems to me that we are losing the art of reasoned debate in favour of 'who can shout the loudest'."

That's because people think that if they can stop you from voicing your point of view; they win the argument.

In reality, regardless of whether you are arguing that 2 + 2 = 5, or 2 = 2 = 4; if you're not willing to allow the other party to explain their reasoning; you loose the debate.

Just a bit of note; there is actually an educated idiot, claiming to be a scientist that postulates that 2 + 2 = 5. He doesn't explain how 2 + 2 = 5; he just claims that it can.

Personally; unless one can explain how 2 + 2 = 5; it is best for one to keep one's mouth shut rather that making oneself look a fool.
